title: The day I learned to ride a bike
summary: Narrate the day you learned to ride a bicycle for the first time.
topic: The day I learned to ride a bike
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about the day you learned to ride a bike. Say how old you were, where you were, and who helped you. Use past simple and past continuous. Describe what was difficult and the moment you finally rode without help.
hintStarters: I was … years old when I learned to ride a bike for the first time…
My … was holding the back of the seat when suddenly…
After falling several times, I finally managed to…
sampleText: I was seven years old when I learned to ride a bike on a wide, quiet street in our neighbourhood in Cape Town. My older sister was the one who helped me. She had been riding bikes since she was five, so she knew exactly what to do. She held the back of the seat with both hands and ran alongside me while I was pedalling. I kept wobbling from side to side and looking down at my feet instead of forward. Every time I looked down, I lost my balance and fell. I scraped my knee twice and cried a little, but I did not want to stop. After about an hour, my sister suddenly let go of the seat without telling me. I was cycling on my own for about ten seconds before I realised she was not holding on. Then I panicked and fell sideways into the grass. But those ten seconds felt amazing. I got back on the bike and tried again. This time I managed to cycle to the end of the street on my own. I squeezed the brakes and stopped right at the corner. My sister was cheering from behind me. I turned the bike around and rode back to her with a huge smile. That evening, I could not stop talking about it at dinner. Learning to ride a bike felt like the most important thing I had ever done.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past continuous interrupted by past simple
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'while + past continuous' để miêu tả điều đang xảy ra khi một sự kiện khác làm gián đoạn nó: 'She ran alongside me while I was pedalling.' Hành động ngắn hơn (ran) dùng past simple; hành động dài hơn (was pedalling) dùng past continuous.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'while + past continuous' for what was in progress when another event happened: 'She ran alongside me while I was pedalling.' The shorter action (ran) uses past simple; the longer background action (was pedalling) uses past continuous.

sampleTranslationVi: Tôi bảy tuổi khi học đi xe đạp trên một con phố rộng, yên tĩnh trong khu dân cư của chúng tôi ở Cape Town. Chị gái tôi là người đã giúp tôi. Chị đã đi xe đạp từ năm tuổi nên biết chính xác phải làm gì. Chị giữ phần sau yên xe bằng cả hai tay và chạy bên cạnh trong khi tôi đang đạp xe. Tôi cứ lảo đảo từ bên này sang bên kia và nhìn xuống chân thay vì nhìn về phía trước. Mỗi lần nhìn xuống là tôi mất thăng bằng và ngã. Tôi trầy đầu gối hai lần và khóc một chút nhưng không muốn dừng. Sau khoảng một tiếng, chị tôi đột nhiên thả yên xe ra mà không nói cho tôi biết. Tôi tự đạp xe được khoảng mười giây trước khi nhận ra chị không còn giữ nữa. Rồi tôi hoảng sợ và ngã xuống bãi cỏ bên cạnh. Nhưng mười giây đó thật tuyệt vời. Tôi lại leo lên xe và thử lại. Lần này tôi tự đạp đến cuối phố. Tôi bóp phanh và dừng lại ngay ở góc đường. Chị đang reo hò phía sau tôi. Tôi quay xe và đạp trở lại chỗ chị với nụ cười rạng rỡ. Tối hôm đó tôi không thể ngừng kể về chuyện đó trong bữa tối. Học đi xe đạp cảm thấy như điều quan trọng nhất tôi từng làm.

mcQuestions.prompt: How old was the writer when learning to ride?
mcQuestions.explanation: The writer says 'I was seven years old when I learned to ride a bike.'

mcQuestions.prompt: Where did the lesson take place?
options: On a quiet street in Cape Town
mcQuestions.explanation: The lesson took place on a wide, quiet street in their neighbourhood in Cape Town.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the location mentioned in the first sentence.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'while + past continuous'?
options: She ran alongside me while I pedalled.
She ran alongside me while I was pedalling.
She was run alongside me while I was pedalling.
She ran alongside me while I pedalling.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'While I was pedalling' correctly uses past continuous for the ongoing background action.

mcQuestions.prompt: What mistake did the writer keep making?
options: Looking down at their feet instead of forward
mcQuestions.explanation: The writer kept looking down at their feet instead of looking forward.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the sentence about where the writer was looking.

mcQuestions.prompt: What did the sister do to help the writer learn?
options: She told the writer to stop looking down
She rode the bike first to show how
She let go of the seat without telling the writer
mcQuestions.explanation: The sister let go of the seat secretly so the writer could cycle alone.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the sentence about what the sister did secretly.

mcQuestions.prompt: How long did the writer cycle alone before realising?
mcQuestions.explanation: The writer cycled alone for about ten seconds before realising the sister had let go.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the sentence about cycling alone.

options: Pressed the brakes to stop the bike
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Squeeze the brakes' means pressing the brake levers to slow or stop a bike.
mcQuestions.hint: What do you do to stop a bicycle?

options: Move unsteadily from side to side
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Wobble' means to move unsteadily, shaking from side to side.
mcQuestions.hint: Think about how a learner cyclist moves before they get their balance.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'I finally ___ to cycle to the end of the street.'
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Managed to' is the correct expression for successfully doing something after effort.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Manage to' + base verb means to succeed after trying hard.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does 'let go' mean in this story?
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Let go of the seat' means to release the grip on the seat — stop holding it.
mcQuestions.hint: Think about what the sister was holding and then released.

mcQuestions.prompt: How did the writer feel at the end of the day?
options: Like it was the most important thing ever done
Disappointed at how long it took
mcQuestions.explanation: The writer says it 'felt like the most important thing I had ever done.'

criticalThinkingQuestion: What skill did you learn as a child that required a lot of practice before you could do it? Why do you think learning physical skills like riding a bike is often easier as a child than as an adult?
